diagrams.title: Triple Constraint (Iron Triangle)
diagrams.description: Visual representation of the three interdependent project constraints
elements: Triangle shape with 'SCOPE' at top vertex
Bidirectional curved arrows between each vertex
Quality shown as inner circle/triangle core
diagrams.purpose: To understand how changing one constraint affects others - fundamental concept in project management decision-making

diagrams.title: Project Lifecycle Wave
diagrams.description: S-curve showing effort and uncertainty across project phases
elements: X-axis: Project Phases (Initiation, Planning, Execution, Monitoring, Closure)
Curved line showing low start, peak at execution, decline at closure
Uncertainty line showing high at start, decreasing over time
diagrams.purpose: To visualize resource allocation and risk reduction throughout project lifecycle

diagrams.title: Stakeholder Power/Interest Grid
diagrams.description: 2x2 matrix for stakeholder classification and management strategy
elements: Four quadrants: High Power/High Interest, High Power/Low Interest, Low Power/High Interest, Low Power/Low Interest
Labels for management strategy in each quadrant
diagrams.purpose: To prioritize stakeholder engagement and communication based on their influence and interest
